nl: In some families, Odell is spelled O'Dell in a mistaken Irish adaptation.
broken_fol: MistakenSpellingOf(nameO'Dell, nameODell) ∧ (∃x∃y(Family(x) ∧ Named(x, nameO'Dell) ∧ (¬(x=y)) ∧ Family(y) ∧ Named(y, nameO'Dell))
corrected_fol: MistakenSpellingOf(nameOdell, nameOdellOriginal) & exists x.(Family(x) & UsesName(x, nameOdell))

nl: Some Whigs speak French.
corrected_fol: exists x.(Whig(x) & SpeakFrench(x))

nl: Some singles of Imagine Dragons have been on Billboard Hot 100.
corrected_fol: exists x.(PopularSingle(imagineDragons, x) & BillboardHot100(x))

nl: Quincy McDuffie can catch some footballs easily.
corrected_fol: exists x.(Football(x) & CanCatchEasily(quincymcduffie, x))

nl: Shafaq-Asiman is a large complex of offshore geological structures in the Caspian Sea.
broken_fol: LargeComplex(shafaq-asiman) ∧ LargeComplex(shafaq-asiman) ∧ Offshore(shafaq-asiman) ∧ GeologicalStructures(shafaq-asiman) ∧ In(shafaq-asiman, caspiansea)
corrected_fol: LargeComplex(shafaqAsiman) & Offshore(shafaqAsiman) & GeologicalStructures(shafaqAsiman) & In(shafaqAsiman, caspianSea)

nl: Palstaves are found in northern, western, and southwestern Europe and are cast in molds.
broken_fol: FoundIn(palstave, northernEurope) ∨ FoundIn(palstave, westernEurope) ∨ FoundIn(palstave, southWesternEurope)) ∧ CastIn(palstave, molds)
corrected_fol: FoundIn(palstave, northernEurope) & FoundIn(palstave, westernEurope) & FoundIn(palstave, southwesternEurope) & CastIn(palstave, molds)

nl: The Emmet Building is a five-story building in Portland, Oregon.
broken_fol: Building(emmetBuilding) ∧ Five-Story(emmetBuilding) ∧ LocatedIn(emmetBuilding, portland) ∧ LocatedIn(portland, oregon))
corrected_fol: Building(emmetBuilding) & FiveStory(emmetBuilding) & LocatedIn(emmetBuilding, portland) & LocatedIn(portland, oregon)

nl: TS Leda was a good passenger and cargo vessel.
corrected_fol: PassengerVessel(tsLeda) & CargoVessel(tsLeda) & Good(tsLeda)

nl: Boney M. had several German #1 singles.
corrected_fol: exists x.(Song(x) & By(x, boneyM) & Number1GermanSingle(x))

nl: Mr. Smith has been to two cities in California.
broken_fol: ∃x ∃y ∀z (¬(x=z) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ ¬(x=y) ∧ City(x) ∧ City(y) ∧ City(z) ∧ California(x) ∧ California(y) ∧ California(z) → Visit(mr.smith, x) ∧ Visit(mr.smith, y) ∧ ¬Visit(mr.smith, z))
corrected_fol: exists x.exists y.(City(x) & InCalifornia(x) & City(y) & InCalifornia(y) & -(x=y) & Visit(mrSmith, x) & Visit(mrSmith, y))

nl: There are five grades in English class: A+, A, B+, B, and C.
broken_fol: GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) ∨ GradeIn(a, englishClass) ∨ GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) ∨ GradeIn(b, englishClass) ∨ GradeIn(c, englishClass) ∧ (GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) → ¬GradeIn(a, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(b, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(c, englishClass)) ∧ (GradeIn(a, englishClass) → ¬GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(b, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(c, englishClass)) ∧ (GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) → ¬GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(a, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(b, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(c, englishClass)) ∧ (GradeIn(b, englishClass) → ¬GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(a, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(c, englishClass)) ∧ (GradeIn(c, englishClass) → ¬GradeIn(aPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(a, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(bPlus, englishClass) ∧ ¬GradeIn(b, englishClass))
corrected_fol: GradeIn(gradeAplus, englishClass) & GradeIn(gradeA, englishClass) & GradeIn(gradeBplus, englishClass) & GradeIn(gradeB, englishClass) & GradeIn(gradeC, englishClass)

nl: If a student gets an A in English class, then his score is greater than 90 but lower than 95.
broken_fol: ∀x ∀y (Student(x) ∧ GetGradeIn(x, a, englishClass) → EnglishClassScore(x, y) ∧ GreaterThan90(y) ∧ LowerThan95(y))
corrected_fol: all x.all y.((Student(x) & GetGradeIn(x, gradeA, englishClass) & EnglishClassScore(x, y)) -> (GreaterThan90(y) & LowerThan95(y)))

nl: Zhang got an A in English class.
corrected_fol: Student(zhang) & GetGradeIn(zhang, gradeA, englishClass)

nl: Oliver plays a different musical instrument from Peter in the concert.
corrected_fol: exists x.exists y.(MusicalInstrument(x) & MusicalInstrument(y) & -(x=y) & PlayIn(oliver, x, concert) & PlayIn(peter, y, concert))

nl: If a soccer player receives one red card in one game, this player will be ejected from the rest of the game.
corrected_fol: all x.((SoccerPlayer(x) & Receive(x, oneRedCard)) -> EjectedFromRestOfGame(x))

nl: In one game, Henry receives one yellow card and one red card.
corrected_fol: Receive(henry, oneYellowCard) & Receive(henry, oneRedCard)

nl: The SAT test is wholly owned and developed by the College Board.
broken_fol: OwnedBy(sAT, collegeBoard) ∧ DevelopedBy(sAT, collegeBoard) ∧ ¬(∃y (¬(y=collegeBoard) ∧ (OwnedBy(sAT, y) ∨ DevelopedBy(sAT, y)))
corrected_fol: OwnedBy(sat, collegeBoard) & DevelopedBy(sat, collegeBoard) & -exists y.(-(y=collegeBoard) & (OwnedBy(sat, y) | DevelopedBy(sat, y)))

nl: A game is played with three stages: red stage, yellow stage, and green stage.
broken_fol: ∃x ∃y ∃y ∃w (Game(x) ∧ StageNumber(x,3) ∧ Stage(y) ∧ Stage(z) ∧ Stage(w) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ ¬(z=w) ∧ ¬(y=w) ∧ Red(y) ∧ Yellow(z) ∧ Green(w))
corrected_fol: exists x.exists y.exists z.exists w.(Game(x) & StageNumber(x, three) & Stage(y) & Stage(z) & Stage(w) & -(y=z) & -(z=w) & -(y=w) & Red(y) & Yellow(z) & Green(w))

nl: Over 400,000 copies of \"1901\" have been sold.
corrected_fol: SoldOver(song1901, n400000)
suite_status: fail:parse_error: free individual variables: ['n400000'] — overlaps with known broken gold
anomaly_notes: suite generation failed: parse_error: free individual variables: ['n400000'] — overlaps with known broken gold

nl: Carrozzeria Colli made car bodies.
corrected_fol: exists x.(CarBody(x) & Made(x, carrozzeriaColli))

nl: Only relay swimmers who participated in the final event at the 1972 Summer Olympics received medals.
corrected_fol: all x.(ReceivedMedal(x) -> (RelaySwimmer(x) & ParticipatedIn(x, finalEvent1972Olympics)))

nl: Zaha Hadid is a British-Iraqi architect, artist, and designer.
corrected_fol: BritishIraqi(zahaHadid) & Architect(zahaHadid) & Artist(zahaHadid) & Designer(zahaHadid)

nl: Luke can make cookies, scrambled eggs, and muffins, but not pasta.
broken_fol: CanMake(luke, cookies) ∧ (CanMake(luke, scrambledEggs) ∧ CanMake(luke, muffins) ∧ ¬CanMake(luke, pasta)
corrected_fol: CanMake(luke, cookies) & CanMake(luke, scrambledEggs) & CanMake(luke, muffins) & -CanMake(luke, pasta)

nl: All people who went to Clay's school that do not have regular 9-5 jobs, work in the entertainment industry as high-profile celebrities.
broken_fol: ∀x (GoTo(x, claysSchool) ∧ ¬(Have(x, y) ∧ Regular(y) ∧ NineToFiveJob(y)) → WorkInAs(x, entertainmentIndustry, highProfileCelebrity))
corrected_fol: all x.((GoTo(x, claysSchool) & -exists y.(Have(x, y) & Regular(y) & NineToFiveJob(y))) -> WorkInAs(x, entertainmentIndustry, highProfileCelebrity))

nl: Some monitors made by LG have a type-c port.
broken_fol: ∃x (Monitor(x) ∧ ProducedBy(x, lG) ∧ Have(x, typeCPort) ∧ (¬(x=y)) ∧ Monitor(y) ∧ ProducedBy(y, lG) ∧ Have(y, typeCPort))
corrected_fol: exists x.(Monitor(x) & ProducedBy(x, lg) & Have(x, typeCPort))

nl: All monitors cheaper than 800 dollars are with a resolution lower than 1080p.
corrected_fol: all x.((Monitor(x) & CheaperThan(x, dollars800)) -> ResolutionLessThan(x, res1080p))

nl: Lily is in James' family; she watches TV series in cinemas.
broken_fol: Customer(lily) ∧ In(lily, jameSFamily ∧ WatchIn(lily, tV, cinema)
corrected_fol: In(lily, jamesFamily) & WatchIn(lily, tvSeries, cinema)

nl: Everyone provided with delicious meals is happy to communicate with each other during the dinner.
broken_fol: ∀x (ProvidedWith(x, deliciousMeal) ∧ ProvidedWith(y, deliciousMeal)  → ∃y ∃z (¬(y=x) ∧ ¬(z=x) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ HappyToCommunicateWithDuringTheDinner(x, y) ∧ HappyToCommunicateWithDuringTheDinner(x, z)))
corrected_fol: all x.all y.((ProvidedWith(x, deliciousMeal) & ProvidedWith(y, deliciousMeal) & -(x=y)) -> HappyToCommunicateDuringDinner(x, y))

nl: Burger is used in the lab computer, and it is written with code and a new version of MacOS.
corrected_fol: UsedIn(burger, labComputer) & WrittenWith(burger, code) & WrittenWith(burger, newMacOsVersion)

nl: Hulu is a multicellular creature that is autotrophic or digests food internally.
corrected_fol: MulticellularCreature(hulu) & (Autotrophic(hulu) | DigestFoodInternally(hulu))
